commit.message:
feat: add upgrade mode to codenook-init

When re-initializing a project that already has CodeNook:
- Preserve runtime data: task-board.json, memory/, config.json, reviews/
- Regenerate framework files: agent profiles, HITL scripts, engine
- Merge config.json: update version, add new keys, keep user settings
- Skip interactive prompts in upgrade mode (use existing config)
- Show preserved vs updated counts in completion summary

Three choices on existing installation:
  Upgrade (recommended) | Fresh install | Cancel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: teetee971 <thierrynaud2009@gmail.com>

feat: auto-maintained wiki — watcher + maintenance agent + lifecycle hooks (#256-258)

The wiki now self-maintains on every code change:

WikiWatcher (#256):
- FNV-1a file hashing, recursive directory walk
- Extension filtering (.rs .ts .py .go .md .json)
- Ignore patterns (node_modules, .git, target)
- Detects created/modified/deleted files via hash diff

WikiMaintenanceAgent (#257):
- Plans actions per change: create/update/delete pages
- Post-pass: rebuild index.md, run lint, append log.md
- execute_action dispatches each action type

WikiAutoTrigger (#258):
- on_session_start: take initial project snapshot
- on_agent_turn_complete: diff → plan → execute → update snapshot
- on_session_end: final maintenance pass
- force_maintenance: manual trigger
- Snapshot taken AFTER actions to prevent re-detection loops

Flow: edit code → agent turn completes → wiki auto-updates
No manual intervention needed.

Tests: 1015 → 1040 (25 new tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add -readrate to per-track ffmpeg for near-realtime output

Instead of modifying MA server's -re flag globally (which would break
Spotify Connect and other realtime plugin sources), throttle our own
per-track ffmpeg input reading to ~1.1x with a 5-second initial burst.

This makes our PCM output arrive at near-realtime rate, compatible
with MA's -re flag on the outer ffmpeg, while eliminating startup
latency via the initial burst.

Applied to both _stream_track and prebuffer _fill paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Continue backend architecture cleanup with domain-specific partial classes and backend infrastructure splits (#328)

* Initial plan

* refactor: split Query.cs into partial class files by domain

Extract 3 domain sections from the 4219-line Query.cs into separate
partial class files:

- Query.Rankings.cs: Player/company rankings, company management,
  game state (GetRankings, GetCompanyRankings, GetMyCompanies,
  GetCompanyBrands, GetCompanySettings, GetGameState, GetStarterIndustries)

- Query.Building.cs: Building lots, exchange, inventory, analytics,
  ledger, power grid, first-sale mission, media houses, marketing stats

- Query.Lending.cs: Bank lending marketplace (loan offers, loans,
  procurement preview, sourcing candidates, unit upgrade info)

Query.cs retains auth/admin/news/stock-exchange, world data/encyclopedia,
and result type classes. Query.Chat.cs remains unchanged.

No logic changes — pure file reorganization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: scholtz <1223439+scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: split Mutation.cs into domain-specific partial class files

Extract building operations, lending, and stock exchange mutations from
the 3455-line Mutation.cs into separate partial class files:

- Mutation.Building.cs (993 lines): StoreBuildingConfiguration,
  CancelBuildingConfiguration, SetBuildingForSale, SetRentPerSqm,
  PurchaseLot, CompleteFirstSaleMilestone, UpdatePublicSalesPrice,
  FlushStorage, ScheduleUnitUpgrade, plus building validation helpers
  and FlushStorageResult/FlushStorageEntry result types.

- Mutation.Lending.cs (397 lines): PublishLoanOffer, UpdateLoanOffer,
  DeactivateLoanOffer, AcceptLoan.

- Mutation.StockExchange.cs (421 lines): BuyShares, SellShares, plus
  share pricing snapshot, trading account resolution, shareholding
  management, and ownership ratio helpers.

- Mutation.cs (1699 lines): Auth, admin, news, company management,
  onboarding, and shared private helpers (GetCurrentTickAsync,
  AddCompanyLedgerEntry, PrepareLotPurchaseAsync, etc.).

All 719 backend tests pass. Pure refactor with no behavioral changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: scholtz <1223439+scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Split large backend GraphQL types into domain-specific partial classes

commit.message:
feat: add pre-spawn intelligence to orchestrator

Add phase-specific context gathering before spawning each subagent:

- Implementer: auto-detect coding conventions (.editorconfig, eslint,
  prettier, etc.), tech stack, existing patterns; ask user on first run
- Reviewer: discover review checklists, use code-review agent type
  for platform-enhanced analysis, collect CI/linter results
- Tester: detect test framework and coverage config
- Designer: scan for architecture docs and ADRs

Update agent profiles:
- Reviewer: accept checklist, conventions, CI results in input contract;
  add checklist results to output template
- Implementer: accept coding conventions and existing patterns;
  match project style in Phase 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tui: fix input newline keys, text color, keyboard enhancement

- Alt+Enter / Ctrl+J as newline keys (work on all terminals without kitty protocol)
- Shift+Enter still works when terminal supports it
- Enable kitty keyboard enhancement (PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags) gracefully
  so Shift+Enter works on supporting terminals (e.g. iTerm2, WezTerm, foot)

perf: borrowed-frame optimization for simple function calls

Skip Arc::clone/drop for code/globals/builtins/constant_cache when
calling simple functions. Instead, bitwise-copy Arcs from the function
object and keep it alive in held_func. Saves 8 atomic operations per
non-recursive function call.

- Added held_func: Option<PyObjectRef> field to Frame
- Added Frame::new_borrowed() constructor using ptr::read
- Updated recycle() to handle held_func before ManuallyDrop
- Updated CallFunction and LoadGlobalCallFunction handlers

commit.message:
Add quick fix code actions for molang optimization diagnostics (#436)

Molang optimization diagnostics (e.g.
`molang.optimization.identity-operation`,
`molang.optimization.constant-folding`) had no associated code actions —
users had to manually rewrite flagged expressions.

## Approach

Per LSP spec, `Diagnostic` objects support a `data` field. Optimization
diagnostics now populate `data: { replacement: string }` with the
suggested rewrite, which the code action provider reads to offer a
one-click fix.

## Changes

- **`Optimization` interface** (`framework.ts`): Added optional
`replacement?: string` field.
- **Optimization rules** (`rules.ts`, `operation-rules.ts`): All rules
that know the replacement now populate it:
- Identity ops (`+0`, `-0`, `*1`, `/1`): replacement =
`ExpressionNode.toString()` of the surviving operand
  - Constant folding: replacement = the folded expression string
  - Both-literal constant result: replacement = the computed value
- **Templates** (`template.ts`): Message factory functions now accept
`(node) => string | Optimization`, allowing rules to return a full
`Optimization` object with `replacement` instead of just a string.
- **Registry** (`registry.ts`): Passes `data: { replacement }` to
`diagnoser.add()` when present.
- **`DiagnosticsBuilder.add()`** (`diagnostics-builder.ts`): Added
optional `data?: unknown` parameter; `InternalDiagnoser` sets it on the
LSP `Diagnostic`; `TestDiagnoser` stores it for test assertions.
- **Code action handler** (`molang/optimization.ts`, new): Reads
`diag.data.replacement` and emits a `QuickFix` `TextEdit.replace` action
titled `"Rewrite to: <replacement>"`.
- **`main.ts`**: Routes all `molang.optimization.*` codes to the new
handler.

## Example

For `variable.smooth_turn * 1`, the diagnoser now emits:
```json
{
  "code": "molang.optimization.identity-operation",
  "data": { "replacement": "variable.smooth_turn" }
}
```
…and the code action provider offers: **"Rewrite to:
variable.smooth_turn"**.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: DaanV2 <2393905+DaanV2@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Implement Universal Antivirus φ-Sentinel (Phase 24) — ELF backend, phi-fingerprint, scan CLI (#55)

* feat(backends): add ELF/PE/Mach-O binary analysis backend

Add elf_light.py backend for parsing native binary formats (ELF, PE,
Mach-O) with zero external dependencies. Extracts structural metrics
per section including Shannon entropy, shellcode detection, W+X section
detection, and stripped binary indicators. Uses Fibonacci distance for
section sizes and phi_ratio for structural harmony analysis.

Register ElfLightBackend in backends/__init__.py and route binary
extensions (.elf, .so, .o, .exe, .dll, .sys, .dylib, .bin) through
the factory in analyseur.py.

commit.message:
feat: byte-accurate progress sync to MA UI and Yandex app

Calculate real playback progress from PCM bytes yielded instead of
relying on Ynison state echoes (which arrive every ~5s and don't
reflect actual audio position).

- _bytes_to_ms: converts PCM byte count to milliseconds
- _sync_progress: pushes elapsed to MA metadata + Ynison every 5s
- _update_metadata: skips Ynison-based elapsed when actively streaming
  (byte-based progress is the source of truth during playback)

Formula: real_progress = seek_ms + (bytes_yielded * 1000) / byte_rate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tui): dynamic task plan panel for sub-agent progress tracking

Add a live task plan panel that renders between messages and status bar:

  ✻ Cooked for 2m 6s · 1 shell running
    7 tasks (4 done, 3 open)
    √ Implement hermes-batch crate
    □ Fix flaky Exa backend test (01:32)

- New taskplan.rs module: TaskPlan, TrackedTask, TaskStatus data model
- Tracks sub-agent lifecycle via AgentSpawned/AgentComplete/AgentTerminated
- Tracks active shell count from Bash/Shell tool start/complete events
- Running tasks show live elapsed time (mm:ss)
- Panel auto-hides when no tasks exist (zero layout cost)
- Integrated into vertical layout between message area and separator
- 7 new tests for task plan logic, 385 total passing

- Build regex from ALLOWED_TYPES to avoid duplication
- Require non-whitespace description (reject 'feat:  ')
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md to clarify PR-title-only validation
